segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens on a brightly lit stage with three judges seated behind a desk adorned with illuminated 'X' symbols, facing a large, engaged audience. He begins his act by holding up a 'COOL MEMORIES!' card, then reveals a Polaroid-style photo of a red rose, while speaking about how photos help define who we are and capture memories. He addresses the judges, naming them as 'Simon, Heidi, Sophia, Howie, Terry and myself,' explaining that he has photos to help them take a mental snapshot of the moment. As uplifting, dramatic background music plays, he talks about shared core memories from childhood, like 'pulling your first all-nighter' and 'your first crush,' eliciting claps and laughter from the audience. Kevin then walks toward the judges, handing a photo to Judge 2, identified as Heidi (woman in yellow top), who smiles and responds 'Hello' and 'Yes' to his instructions. He tells her not to look at it yet, to which she replies 'Okay.' He then hands another photo to Judge 3, identified as Simon (man in dark suit), who reacts with surprise, raising his hand. He also hands a photo to Judge 1, identified as Howie (bald, glasses, black jacket), who shows interest. Kevin instructs Heidi to show the photo to everyone, including the judges and 'the world,' before hiding it. The audience remains visibly engaged, clapping and smiling throughout, as the dynamic lighting and stage effects enhance the theatrical atmosphere. The sequence ends with close-ups of the judges examining the photos, particularly Heidi, who holds up a photo showing a hand reaching into a red cup, while Kevin gestures toward them with a smile, asking them to vividly remember all the details.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with Brynn Cummings, a young girl in a green dress with a white heart pattern, confidently on stage operating a pink and white furry puppet. The puppet, speaking in a high-pitched voice, declares, "Big moment. Are you ready for me to queue the music? I don't wanna sing anymore." Brynn, using a slightly lower voice, responds, "What? This is what we've practiced for weeks." The puppet continues, "I've been thinking about it, and it's done, done. They wanna see something else, something new." Brynn then asks, "But we haven't practiced anything else. What's your plan?" The puppet exclaims, "I've got it! Mentalism!" to which Brynn, in the puppet's voice, reacts with a surprised "Mentalism? Wait, what?" The audience laughs and cheers. Brynn, addressing the puppet as "Reed," says, "I don't mind, Reed. Quit playing around, let's just sing the song. Trust me!" She then instructs, "Take him down to the judges." As Brynn walks towards the judges' table with the puppet, the puppet asks, "Is that allowed?" Judge 2 (woman in yellow top) and Judge 3 (man in dark suit) both respond with "Yeah, yeah!" The stage is lit with dynamic blue and gold, and the judges, including Judge 1 (bald, glasses, black jacket), are seated behind a sleek table with 'X' designs and Sonic-branded cups. The audience is visibly engaged, clapping and smiling. Judge 2 leans forward, laughing, while Judge 3 raises his hand in astonishment. Brynn hands the puppet to Judge 2, who gently touches its fur. Judge 2, impressed, whispers, "He's so good," a sentiment echoed by Judge 3 who adds, "He's good." The puppet, now on the table, expresses worry in its high-pitched voice, "I have a bad feeling about this." Brynn, in her natural voice, reassures it, "Oh, stop worrying." The puppet replies, "Seriously, I don't know what I'm supposed to do." Brynn then suggests, "It's okay, we'll use a deck of cards. Check your pocket." The puppet reacts with a surprised "Wait, what? Ta-da!" Judge 2 picks up a red straw from her Sonic cup, intrigued. Brynn then addresses Judge 2 by name, "Heidi, I'd like you to cut the deck one time," as playful, slightly mysterious music plays in the background, building anticipation for the mentalism act.

segments.integrated_caption: The America's Got Talent stage is set with a vibrant cityscape backdrop and judges Simon, Sofia, Heidi, and Howie at their table, each with a Sonic-branded cup and red buzzer. Mandy Muden, a woman with blonde hair in a leopard-print jacket over a black dress and a large white statement necklace, confidently walks onto the stage carrying a beige handbag. She begins her performance with expressive gestures, moving theatrically as she speaks with a British accent, stating "29" and joking, "It's been a rough couple of years, you know what I mean?" The audience and judges react with laughter and an audible "Oh." Howie, a man in a dark suit, asks, "And uh, what do you do, Mandy?" to which Mandy replies, "I do magic," prompting applause. Sofia, a woman in a yellow top, then asks, "Why AGT?" Mandy responds, "Well, being British, as Simon will back me up to say, I love America!" followed by cheers, and then adds humorously, "Also, I owe money and I have to get out the UK," eliciting more laughter. Simon, a bald man in a black jacket, asks, "What are you going to do with a million dollars if you win?" Mandy quips, "What I do? I'll probably spend it on men," leading to widespread laughter and Sofia exclaiming, "On men!" Howie encourages her, saying, "Okay, we're ready for you. We want to see your magic," and Mandy enthusiastically replies, "Okay, fantastic!" as the audience cheers. Mandy then addresses the crowd, "Right, everybody, are you ready to get into a magic mood?" which is met with loud cheers. She continues, "Okay, America's Got Talent. Look at this. Four strangers ready to judge me. It's like one of those parties where you throw the car keys into the fishbowl," prompting more laughter as she interacts directly with the judges. The segment ends with Mandy near the judges, still animated, as the crowd continues to react enthusiastically.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens on the vibrant America's Got Talent stage, illuminated with blue and orange lights and featuring a cityscape backdrop, as Ryland, a young boy in a teal polo and brown pants, confidently walks towards the judges' panel. He addresses the audience and judges, stating with a clear, articulate voice, "We'll get back to them later. What you're about to see is amazing, but I can't do it on my own. I need help. Luckily, I have lots of friends that love magic just like me." Judge 2, Heidi, in a yellow top, warmly exclaims, "Oh, hello there!" while Judge 3, Howie, in a dark suit, adds a friendly, "Hi friends." Ryland hands a paper to Judge 1, Simon, who smiles and reacts with interest. The camera then cuts to a group of young men in black shirts, bearing names like 'JAKE', 'MARK', 'JUSTIN', 'JORDAN', 'RYAN', 'OM', and 'SKYLER', each holding numbered cards. Ryland stands before them, explaining his act: "I'll let each of you to pick one of my friends, pick one of the numbers, and I'll place it in this holder. You get to choose which position it goes in. There are multiple combinations and the choices are all yours." He then directs Howie, "We'll start with you, Howie. Please name one of my friends." Howie, with a slight hesitation, responds, "Me? Uh, okay, uh, Mark." Ryland asks, "And what would you like, the number 7 or 23?" to which Howie confidently replies, "I'll take 7, it's lucky." When asked, "And where would you like it to go? 1, 2, 3 or 4?" Howie playfully states, "First, 'cause I'm first." Ryland acknowledges, "Okay, so number one. Heidi, your turn. Which of my friends?" Heidi, with a clear voice, chooses, "I choose Om." Ryland then offers, "Would you like 11 or 26?" and Heidi quickly decides, "11." For its placement, Ryland asks, "And where would you like that to go? 2, 3 or 4?" and Heidi responds, "Four." Ryland thanks her, "Thank you, Heidi. Sofia, it's your turn. Which of my friends would you like?" as he continues to direct the performance, placing numbers on a black music stand. The audience watches intently, clapping and smiling, while the judges remain engaged, with Judge 2 and Judge 3 showing amusement and curiosity, and Judge 1 listening attentively.

segments.integrated_caption: The America's Got Talent stage glows with its iconic logo and a vibrant cityscape backdrop as Ryland, a young boy in a teal polo, confidently directs a group of young men in black shirts, each holding numbered cards, from a black music stand. Ryland's clear, enthusiastic voice asks, "Skyler! Would you like 24 or 10?" to which a group member responds, "10!" He then asks, "And where would you like it to go? Two or three?" and the reply is "Two!" After thanking "Sophia," Ryland turns to the judges, asking Judge 3, "And Simon, which of my friends would you like?" Judge 3, identified as Simon Cowell by his distinct British voice, replies, "Jordan." Ryland repeats, "Jordan," then asks, "Would you like one or 15?" Simon chooses, "One." Ryland proposes a "different" task, asking if he'd like to put it in the last position or swap it, to which Simon responds, "Okay," with a slight chuckle. Judge 1, identified as Howie Mandel by his voice, interjects humorously, "He's taking my position," which Simon echoes with a laugh, as the audience erupts in laughter and applause. Ryland thanks Simon, and the applause continues. He then shifts focus, saying, "Now, let's look at the drawings you didn't use," describing them as "all different" and listing "Lego, Dragons, We've even got burgers." Simon confirms with a "Yes." Ryland then presents the chosen hand-drawn cards, identifying them as "A snake," "A slice of lime," "A house," and "Some mountains," as the judges watch intently, with Simon pointing and engaging, and the lively audience continues to clap amidst the upbeat, suspenseful background music.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of Judge 2 (woman in yellow top) smiling broadly at the judges' table, as Ryland, a young boy in a teal polo shirt, stands center stage. He gestures towards a black music stand displaying four colorful hand-drawn cards: a snake, a lime, a house, and mountains. Ryland's clear, articulate voice explains, "Each of you had a free choice of which drawing you wanted, and more importantly, where to put it." He then details how "The snake looks like an S, the lime looks like a C, the house looks like an A, and the mountains look like an M," concluding, "That spells the word scam." Judge 1 (bald, glasses, black jacket) confirms with a verbal "Right?", and Judge 3 (man in dark suit) adds a "Yeah," as Ryland declares, "That can be our magic word," prompting laughter from the audience and judges. Ryland then instructs, "Everyone shout scam as loud as they can on the count of three. One, two, three!" The audience and judges loudly exclaim "SCAM!" followed by applause and laughter. Ryland continues, "But what if I told you that we knew what the magic word would be before you even picked it?" He instructs the judges to take their drawings "out of the envelope and show it to everyone." As upbeat, suspenseful music begins to play, Judge 1, Judge 2, and Judge 3 are seen holding up their matching cards—snake, lime, house, and mountains—to an audible "Ooh!" from the audience. Ryland confirms, "As you can see, they match," as the stage, illuminated with dynamic blue and orange lighting and a large star-shaped backdrop, is filled with the sound of enthusiastic applause and cheers from the engaged audience, clearly enjoying the performance.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a wide shot of the America's Got Talent judges' table, featuring Judge 1 (Simon, bald, glasses, black jacket), Judge 2 (Heidi, woman in yellow top), and Judge 3 (Sofia, man in dark suit), seated behind a sleek white table adorned with Sonic-branded cups and red buzzers, all against a vibrant cityscape backdrop displaying the show’s logo. Upbeat, suspenseful music plays as Magic Mike Jacobson, a man with dark hair and a beard, wearing a mustard-yellow jacket, stands center stage, using expressive hand gestures as he addresses the audience, his voice slightly nervous but confident as he states, "we'll see what happens." Judge 2 (Heidi) lightheartedly interjects, "No pressure!", to which Magic Mike responds, "Yeah, no big deal. But actually a huge deal for me," admitting he's "a little nervous" but "so excited to be here." Judge 1 (Simon) encourages him, asking, "Are you nervous?" and wishing him "the best of luck, buddy," prompting cheers and applause from the captivated audience. Magic Mike then walks confidently toward the judges, engaging them with animated movements, explaining he will share something "every magician has, but no magician will show you." He reveals an "invisible deck of cards," acknowledging it's "a bit hard to see in this light," and announces his plan to "burn the invisible deck from 52 all the way down to one." As he approaches, he performs a magic trick, producing a small red and blue object from his hand, which he hands to Judge 1 (Simon). The judges react with curiosity and amusement, leaning forward to examine the object, with Judge 3 (Sofia) showing a focused expression and Judge 2 (Heidi) smiling. Magic Mike then directly addresses Judge 1 (Simon), saying, "Simon, you're going to be a hero," and mentions that the outcome "depends on what Heidi and Sofia decide," instructing Judge 2 (Heidi) to "take that match." segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a close-up of Judge 2, a woman in a bright yellow off-the-shoulder top with long blonde hair and sparkling jewelry, holding a lit matchstick near her face with a focused, slightly amused expression as suspenseful, upbeat music plays. Magic Mike Jacobson, a man with dark hair and a beard in a mustard-yellow jacket, stands on a stage with a vibrant, illuminated cityscape backdrop, gesturing expressively as he performs a magic trick. He approaches the judges' table, where Judge 2, Judge 3 (a man in a dark suit), Judge 1 (bald, glasses, black jacket), and Judge 3's counterpart (a woman in a red dress) are seated with Sonic-branded cups. Magic Mike explains, "Let's just say there's two groups, number cards and face cards. We're going to decide whether you want to start to burn all the numbers or all the faces." He instructs Judge 2, "Blow it out when you're ready." Judge 2 asks, "Am I telling you?" to which Magic Mike replies, "Yes, please." She then declares, "All the faces," and blows out the matchstick flame, receiving a small red and blue object from Magic Mike. He states, "The faces are now in the fire. You can pass the box over to Sophia." The camera cuts to the woman in the red dress, who also holds a lit matchstick and blows it out with a smile after Magic Mike tells her, "Sophia, take your match." He continues, "Left in the fire, we got Jacks, Queens, Kings. The only way to divide those up would be, yeah, men and women. Only to burn that match and tell us, do you want to burn all the men, do you want to burn all the women to a crisp?" She quickly responds, "Oh, the men." Magic Mike exclaims, "That was a very quick decision! Go ahead and you blow it out. And you can pass that to Simon. You're going to save one of the remaining cards for burning. All we have left are the four Queens." He lists, "Queen of Hearts, Queen of Spades, Queen of Clubs, Queen of..." as the audience watches intently, some clapping and smiling, before erupting in applause and cheers as Magic Mike smiles and gestures toward them.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with Magic Mike Jacobson, a magician in a mustard-yellow jacket, performing a close-up magic trick on a stage lit with vibrant blue and orange hues, featuring a star-shaped backdrop and cityscape design. He reveals a playing card, the Jack of Diamonds, manipulating it skillfully, while ultimately declaring the trick was to "save the Queen of Diamonds." He continues, "I want you to make sure it's singed, like it's been at a bit of a fire. You guys could have saved whatever you wanted to, but you and you and you decided together to save the Queen of Diamonds." The judges, including Judge 3 and Judge 2, show expressions of amazement and amusement, while the audience claps and smiles, their engagement culminating in enthusiastic applause and cheers at the trick's reveal. segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with Judge 1, a bald man with glasses and a black jacket, adjusting his microphone and gesturing, as Magic Mike Jacobson, a man with dark hair and a beard in a mustard-yellow jacket, begins a close-up magic trick on stage. Magic Mike's voice is heard instructing, "Now, take the card, slide it right back in. Put that all back in the box. And then throw it right back up to me whenever you're ready. Perfect." He skillfully shuffles a deck of cards, drawing the judges' attention. The audience reacts with cheers and applause, prompting Magic Mike to playfully remark, "Now, you... just just minor miracles, it's fine." He then addresses Judge 1 by name, "Howie, you shuffled the deck. You picked a card, you all saw it. For the very first time. What's the card that you picked?" Judge 1, identified as Howie, responds with a clear voice, "Ten of Clubs." Magic Mike clarifies, "I want to clarify something. You and I have never met, correct? Never. Nobody came up to you before the show, said anything? For the very first time when I open this up, you said the Ten of Clubs, correct?" Howie confirms, "I did." Magic Mike builds suspense, explaining, "Because every single card in here is face up, except for one." Dramatic, suspenseful music swells as Judge 2, a woman in a yellow top, reacts with a soft "Uh-oh." Magic Mike reveals a single card, the Jack of Diamonds, holding it up and asking Howie, "One card with a signature on it. Howie, can you confirm, is that your signature?" Howie, surprised, exclaims, "It is." Magic Mike then asks, "Will you flip that over for me?" to which Howie responds with disbelief and excitement, "Are you kidding me?" Magic Mike delivers the final line, "Is that your card?" The audience erupts in massive cheers, applause, and gasps. Magic Mike thanks them, "Thank you very much," as the vibrant blue and orange stage lighting and cityscape backdrop enhance the theatrical atmosphere, with Sonic-branded cups visible on the judges' table.
